unit_checks.expected_behavior: returns {"ok": false, "reason": "missing ddbId"} when event.detail.ddbId is absent or empty
reads the record identified by event.detail.ddbId and returns {"ok": true, "ddbId": ddb_id} on success
propagates runtime failures into a failure payload containing ok=false, the ddbId when available, and a stringified error
raises or fails deterministically when the backing record is missing, matching the legacy RuntimeError behavior
preserves transformation order: load request JSON, transform via TransformerFactory.transform_to_filer_json, persist mergedJSON and transformedJSON, then update transformed status
invokes shared error handling side effects that mark the record as EXCEPTION and trigger notification delivery
accepts events with extra fields without rejecting them, as long as detail.ddbId is present
treats null, empty string, or whitespace-only ddbId as missing and returns {"ok": false, "reason": "missing ddbId"}

integration_checks.verification: Azure implementation must construct a real Azure data client lazily and mockable, with tests intercepting the client factory and verifying a single record lookup by ID
tests must verify writes for mergedJSON, transformedJSON, status.transformed, and finalStatus occur through the same repository/adapter owner
tests must verify an Azure message/event publisher is invoked once with a payload containing ddbId for the validator destination
tests must verify the exception path updates record state to EXCEPTION and emits the configured notification side effect without reaching real Azure or Slack endpoints

validation_rules: missing detail.ddbId returns ok=false and reason=missing ddbId
null, empty, or whitespace-only detail.ddbId returns ok=false and reason=missing ddbId
event payload must be accepted without requiring extra fields
success response must preserve ok=true and echo the ddbId
reason string must match legacy contract
runtime failure payload must include an error string
ddbId must be included when it was available from the input

required_code_constraints: no aws imports anywhere in production code or tests
no module-scope Azure SDK client construction
tests must mock all Azure SDK network calls
HTTP trigger only if the source contract is explicitly re-mapped; otherwise preserve event-driven trigger semantics
every public function must have type annotations
no bare except and no NotImplementedError or pass stubs in production code

bicep_constraints: Bicep must declare the Azure Function App
Bicep must declare a storage account required by Azure Functions
Bicep must declare the selected backing data resource (Cosmos DB or Table Storage) used by the migrated repository
Bicep must declare any downstream eventing resource used for validator publication
Bicep must enable managed identity and Application Insights
Bicep app settings must reference Key Vault for secrets where applicable

coverage_constraints: coverage must not drop below the current baseline
every public function and the decorated entrypoint must have at least one test
